figure: Dowels\n(0100) 2 feet long, deformed, #3–#6\n(0110) 12 inches long, smooth, #3
Minimum suggested crew size: four skilled laborers.

Notes:
1. Reinforcing bar is taken off by LF and converted to pounds or, for large quantities, tons.
Reinforcing bar is available in stock lengths of 20 feet. Refer to Figure 5-C-1 for sizes and
weights of reinforcing bar.
2. The estimator should list separately each size (by bar designation) and shape required. If
specifications and drawings don’t specify an overlap, the bars should be lapped not fewer
than 32 times the bar diameter, or not less than 24 inches. Add 10 percent for overlap and
waste.
3. Placement of reinforcing steel includes handling into place, tying, supporting, and any
cutting which becomes necessary at the site, such as cutting around imbedded items or
cutting stock lengths of straight bars to fit slab dimensions.
4. Man-hour estimates are based on all reinforcing steel being shop fabricated (cut to length
and bent ready to place in the structure).
5. If reinforcing steel is to be welded in place, use a 1.5 multiplier.
6. Order three 4-pound rolls of the wire for each ton of rebar (16-gauge black annealed wire).
